title: 7 Time-Tested Herbal Decoctions for Calming Coughing Fits

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving coughing.
Saponins are good for coughing fits because they have natural expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and cough up phlegm.

Flavonoids are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and fight off infections in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviate coughing.
Triterpenoids are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory and antitussive properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and calm the coughing reflex.

Licorinic acid is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm irritated throats and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving the discomfort of a cough.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, has been used for centuries to soothe coughs. The root of the plant contains a sweet compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When you make a decoction from licorice root, the glycyrrhizin is released into the liquid, creating a soothing effect on the throat and airways. This can help to calm a cough, making it less frequent and less severe. The anti-inflammatory properties of glycyrrhizin can also reduce swelling in the throat and lungs, further reducing the severity of a cough. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help to fight off infections that can cause coughing fits. When consumed as a decoction, licorice root can provide quick relief from coughs, making it a popular natural remedy for this common health issue.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing herbal decoction from Verbascum thapsus, also known as Mullein, to help with coughing fits, start by gathering fresh or dried leaves and flowers of the plant. If using fresh, rinse the leaves and flowers in cold water and pat them dry with a clean cloth. Next, combine one teaspoon of the dried leaves and flowers or two teaspoons of the fresh ones in a saucepan with one cup of boiling water. Reduce the heat to a low simmer and let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes, allowing the plant's properties to infuse into the water. After the steeping time, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup, discarding the solids. You can now drink the decoction warm or at room temperature, ideally 2-3 times a day, to help alleviate coughing fits and soothe your throat.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Alkylamides are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the airways and reduce swelling, making it easier to breathe and cough up mucus.
Polyphenols are good for coughing fits because they have strong anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the throat, reducing irritation and coughing.

Iridoids are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help reduce swelling in the throat, loosen mucus, and make it easier to cough up, providing relief from coughing fits.
Flavonoids are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and recover from a cough.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea decoctions have been used for centuries to help with coughing fits. The plant's roots and flowers contain special compounds that help reduce inflammation in the body. When you have a cough, your airways can become swollen and irritated, leading to more coughing. Echinacea purpurea decoctions work by decreasing this inflammation, which helps your airways relax and become less irritated. This can help ease the urge to cough and make it easier to breathe. The decoctions also contain antioxidants that help fight off infections and boost your immune system, which can help your body recover from illness faster. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea decoctions have expectorant properties, which means they help bring up mucus and other fluids from your lungs, making it easier to cough up and clear out your airways. By reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and helping to clear out mucus, Echinacea purpurea decoctions can provide relief from coughing fits and help you feel better.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a herbal decoction from Verbascum thapsus, commonly known as mullein, which helps to soothe and calm coughing fits, start by gathering 2-3 tablespoons of dried mullein flowers and leaves. Rinse the dried herbs with cold water to remove any dirt or impurities, then place them in a saucepan. Add 1 cup of water and bring the mixture to a boil over medium heat. Once boiling, reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse and release their medicinal properties. After the decoction has cooled slightly, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding the solids. You can drink the decoction as is, or add a bit of honey to sweeten the flavor and make it easier to swallow. It's recommended to drink the decoction 2-3 times a day to help alleviate coughing fits and promote overall respiratory health.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Eucalyptol is good for coughing fits because it helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of coughs.
Quercetin is good for coughing fits because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm and soothe the airways, reducing the frequency and severity of coughs.
Catechins are good for coughing fits because they have strong anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe an irritated throat and reduce inflammation, which can calm and stop a cough.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus decoctions can be helpful for people who have frequent coughing fits. This is because it has a special oil called eucalyptol, which is a natural expectorant. An expectorant is a substance that helps bring up mucus and other things that might be stuck in your chest. When you have a cough, it's often because your body is trying to get rid of something that's stuck in your lungs. Eucalyptus globulus decoctions help by loosening this stuck mucus, making it easier to cough up. This can be especially helpful for people with colds or flu, or for those who have respiratory problems like asthma or bronchitis. When you drink a decoction of eucalyptus globulus, the eucalyptol in it can help ease congestion and make breathing easier. This can help reduce the frequency and severity of coughing fits, giving you some relief from the discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Verbascum thapsus herbal decoction to help with coughing fits, you will need the dried roots of the plant. Start by rinsing 2-3 teaspoons of the dried roots with water, and then place them in a pot with 2 cups of water. Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for 10-15 minutes. Strain the liquid into a separate container and discard the solids. You can then sweeten the decoction with a bit of honey if desired. This herbal tea is said to help soothe and calm a cough, making it easier to breathe and feel more comfortable. It's best to drink the decoction when your symptoms are at their worst, and you can also store it in the fridge for up to 24 hours to drink at a later time. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have underlying health conditions or take medications.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Thymol is good for coughing fits because it has natural antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the throat, reducing irritation and congestion that can cause coughing.

Roslavmarinic acid is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving the discomfort of a cough.
Carvacrol is good for coughing fits because it has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the irritated throat, reducing the severity and frequency of coughing.
Tannins are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving coughing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, is a plant that has been used for centuries to help with coughs and colds. Its decoctions, made by steeping the leaves in hot water, are particularly effective in soothing and calming the throat and lungs. The active ingredients in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow. When we have a cough, our body is trying to get rid of mucus and other foreign particles that are irritating our airways. Thyme decoctions can help to loosen and clear out this mucus, making it easier to cough up and reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits. Additionally, thyme has a natural expectorant property, which means it helps to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel from the body.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Verbascum thapsus herbal decoction that helps with coughing fits, start by gathering fresh or dried Verbascum thapsus leaves and flowers. You will need about one teaspoon of the herb for every cup of water you plan to use. If you are using fresh leaves and flowers, simply chop them up finely and add them to a saucepan. If you are using dried Verbascum thapsus, you can add it directly to the saucepan. Next, add one cup of water to the saucepan and bring the mixture to a boil. Once boiling, reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture cook for about 5-10 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced slightly and the flavors have been released. Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding the solids. You can now drink the decoction hot, or let it cool down and drink it at room temperature. To make it more soothing, you can add a bit of honey or lemon juice to taste. It's recommended to drink the decoction 2-3 times a day to help alleviate coughing fits and soothe your throat.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols are good for coughing fits because they help reduce inflammation in the body and act as an expectorant, making it easier to cough up mucus and relieve congestion.
Shogaols are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and alleviating coughing.
Zingerone is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the irritated throat, reducing the severity and frequency of coughs.

Polyphenols are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and calm the airways, making it easier to breathe and relax during a coughing fit.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has been used for centuries to help soothe coughs. The active compounds in ginger, like gingerols and shogaols, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways. This can make it easier to breathe and swallow, which is especially helpful when you're experiencing a coughing fit. Ginger also has natural expectorant properties, meaning it helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and get rid of the irritant that's causing the cough. When made into a decoction, ginger can be easily absorbed into the body, providing quick relief from a cough. Additionally, ginger has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off underlying infections that may be contributing to the cough. Drinking a warm ginger decoction can provide instant comfort and relief from a coughing fit, making it a popular natural remedy for this common symptom.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Verbascum thapsus herbal decoction to help with coughing fits, start by gathering the dried roots and leaves of the plant, also known as mullein. Rinse the dried material in a fine-mesh strainer under cold running water, and then chop them into small pieces. In a saucepan, combine 2 teaspoons of the chopped mullein with 2 cups of boiling water, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their medicinal properties. Next, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer into a cup, and discard the solids. Add a sweetener like honey if needed to make the decoction more palatable. Once cooled, Verbascum thapsus decoction can be consumed 2-3 times a day, ideally before meals, to help alleviate coughing fits and provide relief to the respiratory system.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the irritated airways and reduce the severity of coughing.
Menthol is good for coughing fits because it helps to ease congestion and calm irritated airways by creating a cooling sensation that reduces inflammation and soothes the throat.
Catechins are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and cough up mucus.
Tannins are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the irritated tissues in the throat, reducing the frequency and severity of coughing.
Polyphenols are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relax.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita decoctions, also known as peppermint, can help with coughing fits because it has soothing properties. When you drink peppermint, the coolness of the mint can calm your throat and reduce the urge to cough. The menthol in peppermint also helps to thin out mucus, making it easier to cough up. This can be especially helpful when you have a cold or flu, and your airways are congested. Additionally, peppermint has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the throat and make it feel more comfortable. Drinking peppermint decoctions can also help to ease congestion and open up your airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the frequency of coughing fits.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Verbascum thapsus herbal decoction to help with coughing fits, start by gathering the roots and leaves of the plant, also known as mullein. Rinse the roots and leaves thoroughly with water to remove any dirt or debris. Next, combine 2-3 tablespoons of the mullein roots and leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let it steep for 5-10 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their medicinal properties into the water. Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding the solids. You can add a bit of honey to sweeten the decoction if desired. Finally, drink the warm mullein decoction as needed to help soothe and calm coughing fits, and to ease congestion and inflammation in the respiratory tract.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Saponins are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving coughing symptoms.
Flavonoids are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated airways, reducing the severity and frequency of coughing episodes.
Tannins are good for coughing fits because they have natural antitussive properties that help reduce inflammation and soothe the throat, thereby providing relief from persistent coughing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus, also known as mullein, is a plant that has been used for centuries to treat coughing fits. The leaves and flowers of the plant are used to make a decoction, which is a liquid medicine. The decoction is made by boiling the plant in water. The steam from the decoction can help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up. This can be especially helpful for people who have a persistent cough or who are experiencing a coughing fit. The anti-inflammatory properties of mullein can also help to soothe and calm the throat, reducing discomfort and pain. Additionally, the plant contains compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the lungs, which can help to clear up infections that may be causing the cough. When made into a decoction and inhaled, the vapors from the mullein can help to calm the cough and make it easier to breathe.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Verbascum thapsus herbal decoction for coughing fits, you'll need some dried Verbascum thapsus leaves, roots, and stems, as well as a large pot, water, and a strainer. First, fill the large pot with about 4-5 cups of water and bring it to a boil. Next, add 2-3 tablespoons of dried Verbascum thapsus leaves, roots, and stems to the boiling water and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes. The longer it simmers, the stronger the decoction will be, but be careful not to burn the mixture. Once the decoction has finished simmering, turn off the heat and let it cool down for a few minutes. Then, carefully pour the decoction through a strainer into a separate container, making sure to squeeze the herbs to extract as much liquid as possible. Finally, drink the decoction warm, 2-3 times a day, to help soothe and calm your coughing fits. It's essential to note that before using any herbal remedy, consult with a healthcare professional or a qualified herbalist to ensure it's safe for your specific health needs.

intro_desc: A persistent cough can disrupt your daily life, making it hard to sleep, work, or even enjoy simple activities. One effective way to alleviate this issue is by using herbal decoctions, specifically those made from Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, and Echinacea purpurea. Glycyrrhiza glabra has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Echinacea purpurea, on the other hand, is known for its immune-boosting properties, which can help fight off underlying infections that may be causing the cough. By combining these two herbs in a decoction, you can create a natural remedy that addresses the root cause of the cough, rather than just masking the symptoms.  Fixing the issue of a persistent cough is crucial because it can lead to more serious health problems, such as bronchitis, pneumonia, or even asthma attacks. If left untreated, a persistent cough can also cause fatigue, anxiety, and stress, further affecting your quality of life. By addressing the issue with herbal decoctions, you can improve your sleep, increase your energy levels, and regain control over your daily activities. This, in turn, can lead to a better work-life balance, stronger relationships, and a more positive outlook on life.
supplementary_best: For coughing fits, a combination of Eucalyptus globulus, Thymus vulgaris, and Glycyrrhiza glabra is effective. Eucalyptus globulus helps to clear mucus from the lungs, reducing congestion and making it easier to breathe. Thymus vulgaris, or thyme, has antimicrobial properties that fight off infections causing the cough. Glycyrrhiza glabra, licorice root, soothes the throat and reduces inflammation. This combination is best because it addresses multiple aspects of coughing: congestion, infection, and inflammation. Additionally, Eucalyptus globulus and Thymus vulgaris have a decongestant and expectorant effect, helping to bring up mucus and phlegm.
supplementary_avoid: If you have coughing fits, it's best to avoid herbal decoctions that can irritate your throat or airways. One such herb is 'Aristolochia clematitidis', also known as birthwort. This plant contains aristolochic acid, which can cause kidney damage and worsen respiratory problems. Consuming it can also lead to severe stomach pain and bleeding.  Another herb to avoid is 'Krameria triandra', known as rat's tail or snake root. It contains kallol, a chemical that can irritate your throat and airways, making coughing fits worse. Some studies also suggest that krameria can cause allergic reactions and interact with certain medications.  'Sassafras albidum', or sassafras, is another herb to be cautious of. Its roots and bark contain safrole, a compound that can cause respiratory problems and make coughing fits worse. Excessive consumption of sassafras has also been linked to liver damage and other health issues.  Lastly, 'Tussilago farfara', or coltsfoot, contains a chemical called tussilagine, which can cause allergic reactions and interact with certain medications. While it's sometimes used to relieve coughs, it's not recommended for people with asthma or other respiratory issues, as it can make symptoms worse.  These herbal decoctions should be used with caution or avoided altogether if you have persistent coughing fits. It's always best to consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
supplementary_other_remedies: Honey has been used for centuries to soothe sore throats and calm coughs. When you swallow, the sticky liquid coats the throat and chest, helping to reduce irritation and inflammation. This can bring quick relief from coughing fits, especially when the cough is caused by a sore throat or bronchitis. In addition, honey has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off infections that cause coughs. Some people also find that warm lemon water can help ease a cough, as the citric acid in the lemon juice helps to break down mucus and promote drainage. This can be especially helpful when you have a cold or sinus infection. Another natural remedy is saltwater gargle, where you mix salt with warm water and gargle it several times a day. This can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the throat, making it less likely to trigger a coughing fit.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience coughing fits often have respiratory issues, such as bronchitis or pneumonia. These conditions can be caused by bacterial or viral infections, and can lead to other ailments like shortness of breath, chest pain, and fatigue.  Medicinal herbs can help alleviate these symptoms. For example, thyme is known for its antibacterial properties, which can help fight off bacterial infections that cause bronchitis. Eucalyptus oil, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe.  Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce fever and alleviate chest pain, while slippery elm can help soothe irritated mucous membranes in the throat and lungs. Licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe.  People with coughing fits may also experience digestive issues like nausea or loss of appetite, which can be caused by the body's stress response to the illness. Herbs like peppermint and ginger can help alleviate nausea and support digestion.  Some people may also experience stress or anxiety related to their illness, which can exacerbate symptoms. Herbs like passionflower and lemon balm can help calm the nervous system and promote relaxation.
